creative_brief: Clean, minimal design. Open with the quoted objection in large italic text as a visual anchor. Single green CTA button below the final line. No hero image — let the copy carry the weight. Mobile-first layout with generous white space.
subject: "I'll just invest when the campaign opens"
preheader: That's what everyone says. Here's why it won't work the way you think.
body:
"I'll just invest when the campaign opens — why do I need to join a waitlist?"

I hear this almost every week. And honestly, it's a fair question.

Here's the part most people don't realise.

When a crowdfunding round goes live on Birchal, hundreds of people hit the page at once. The ones who prepared — who already locked in VIP access — get first allocation, priority updates, and a direct line to me before anyone else sees the offer.

The ones who waited? They refresh the page and hope there's room.

FarmThru isn't a stock tip or a tech play. It's a farm-direct grocery store. Real food from real farms across NSW — roughly 15 to 20 partner growers and suppliers — collected from our Brookvale hub, not shipped 2,400km in a refrigerated truck.

No subscription. No lock-in. You order when you need to, collect Monday to Friday, and actually know where your food came from.

That's the business. And we're opening it up so everyday families can own a piece of it.

The VIP list is how you get in first. It costs $5, fully refundable, and it holds your spot ahead of the public round. You also get exclusive Q&A access and priority updates as we build toward launch.

Minimum investment starts at $50. Maximum is $10,000 per investor. But none of that matters if you're not on the list when the round opens.

I'm not going to pretend this is the last chance you'll ever get. But I will tell you this — VIP spots are finite, and every week the list grows. The people joining now are the ones who'll have options later.

Don't be the person refreshing the page.

→ Lock in your $5 refundable VIP spot now

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
